channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Russell puts Mercedes on pole at Las Vegas and Verstappen nips Norris in championship battle
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — George Russell put Mercedes on the pole for the Las Vegas Grand Prix in an upset over teammate Lewis Hamilton, who had been considered the favorite but struggled in Friday night's final qualifying session.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jai'Den Thomas leads No. 23 UNLV to a 27-16 victory over San Jose State
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Jai'Den Thomas ran for 135 yards and the go-ahead touchdown in the third quarter to help No. 23 UNLV keep its conference title hopes alive with a 27-16 victory over San Jose State on Friday night. The Rebels (9-2, 5-1 Mountain West, No.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Austin Hays, Kyle Finnegan, Brendan Rodgers and Jordan Romano among 62 new free agents
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Outfielder Austin Hays and right-hander Kyle Finnegan — both former All-Stars — and second baseman Brendan Rodgers were among 62 players who became free agents Friday when their teams failed to offer 2025 contracts.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SF Giants avoid arbitration with all five non-tender candidates
item.description: The Giants announced on Friday that they've agreed to one-year deals with Mike Yastrzemski and Austin Warren, as well as tendered contracts to Tyler Rogers, Camilo Doval and LaMonte Wade Jr
item.dc:creator: Justice delos Santos, The Mercury News

item.title: Royals acquire second baseman Jonathan India from Reds for right-hander Brady Singer
item.description: The Kansas City Royals have acquired second baseman Jonathan India from the Cincinnati Reds, adding a dependable leadoff hitter and middle infielder to play alongside star shortstop Bobby Witt Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Adam Schefter says wife 'didn't appreciate' raunchy story about breaking news
item.description: Adam Schefter told a story about breaking news shortly after a sexual encounter, and he said his wife was not a fan of that story being made public.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Purdy, Bosa out for Green Bay matchup, Allen steps up as QB
item.description: (KRON) – San Francisco 49ers head coach Kyle Shanahan announced in a press conference Friday that quarterback Brock Purdy will be out for Sunday’s game against the Green Bay Packers. Purdy injured his right shoulder in last Sunday's loss to the Seattle Seahawks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: NHL player ruptured testicle after taking puck to groin
item.description: Minnesota Wild player Mats Zuccarello will miss three to four weeks with a ruptured testicle after taking a puck to the groin during a game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cowboys' $240M star Dak Prescott admits to crying over horrendous season as team faces constant mockery
item.description: Dak Prescott revealed he cried after the Cowboys' recent loss to the Houston Texans as critics of the franchise mock the team relentlessly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alyssa Nakken, first full-time female coach in MLB history, leaving Giants to join Guardians
item.description: Alyssa Nakken, the first woman to coach in an MLB game, is leaving the San Francisco Giants to join the Cleveland Guardians.
item.dc:creator: Tom Withers, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Martin Slumbers hangs his hat on making British Open big and promoting women's golf
item.description: Martin Slumbers could not have witnessed greater theater than his first British Open in charge of the R&A, the record-setting duel between Henrik Stenson and Phil Mickelson at Royal Troon. His only concern in 2016 was the audience.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rafael Nadal retired after the Davis Cup. It's a rare team event in tennis
item.description: MALAGA, Spain (AP) — Rafael Nadal wanted to play his last match before retiring in Spain, representing Spain and wearing the red uniform used by Spain's Davis Cup squad.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CMU women's soccer "feeling pretty good" ahead of NCAA tournament
item.description: Colorado Mesa women’s soccer is in. The Mavs received an at-large bid into the Division II NCAA tournament.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Teen Paves the Way for Female Hockey Players
item.description: Chloe Jezek, a 16-year-old high school hockey player in North County, is paving the way for other girls in the male-dominated sport.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: Bidding war set to begin for star right fielder Juan Soto
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- After meeting with Juan Soto, Yankees managing general partner Hal Steinbrenner is unsure of their chances of resigning the star right fielder. Steinbrenner added that it was a very honest back and forth and that it lasted a couple of hours.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Bulls great Bob Love dead at 81 after battle with cancer
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — An all-star for the Chicago Bulls in the 1970's, franchise great Bob Love has died at the age of 81 after a long battle with cancer, the team announced Monday. Love played from 1968 to 1976 with the Chicago Bulls and was affectionately nicknamed "Butterbean" for his kind demeanor.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Column: Once Eberflus is gone, it's time to find a true culture setter
item.description: The Chicago Bears have lost three games in a row despite having a positive turnover margin, and need a head coach who can galvanize his roster to eat a punch early and rise to the occasion when the game is on the line.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Sengun records triple-double, VanVleet scores 28 in Rockets 143-107 rout of Bulls
item.description: Fred VanVleet scored a season-high 28 points, Alperen Sengun had 20 points, 11 rebounds and 11 assists for his second triple-double this season and the Houston Rockets routed the Chicago Bulls 143-107 on Sunday night for their fifth straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Report: Kris Byrant's Lamborghini Huracan stolen from Colorado home
item.description: Kris Bryant's Lamborghini Huracan was stolen by a multi-state vehicle theft ring, but was recovered by police in Las Vegas after they traced the stolen vehicle to a home in the city.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jazz unable to hold fourth quarter lead in loss to Kings
item.description: SACRAMENTO (ABC4 Sports) - After scoring 60 points in a losing effort the night before, De'Aaron Fox scored 49 points to lead the Sacramento Kings to a 121-117 win over the Utah Jazz on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Morgan, San Juan, Beaver win state football titles
item.description: CEDAR CITY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - In a battle of unbeatens, Morgan High proved to be the last team standing in the 3A high school football playoffs. The Trojans beat Richfield in a defensive battle Saturday night at Southern Utah University, 13-2.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
